title: Stepping In/Spilling Out
description:
### NB! Stepping in/Spilling out will be presented on April 25th from 11:00–11:50 AM in the Dance Pavilion at Karens Minde Kulturhus as part of Grønne Dage. Admission is free. The performance is in English, but it can also be experienced without understanding everything that is being said. The performance is open to all; however, children must be accompanied by an adult.

Stepping in… Spilling out: across your parent’s carpet, stepping into the cypher, stepping in solidarity, stepping with strangers in the club. Collaboration with French percussionist Rémy Gouffault, this work takes a walk along the personal history of Mark’s dance practice through the foundational act of stepping; the different people, communities, histories and politics that collide with Mark’s body through movement. Beginning on an oversized drawing of his family home carpet, using movement, conversations, participation and music, Mark and Rémy guide you through layers of landscapes stepping through; parties, protests, rituals, mosh pits and gas clouds.

title: mountainbuilding
description:
In *Mountainbuilding*, the movements of the body meet the slow transformations of the mountains. The work adapts to its surroundings and, in this version, becomes anchored in the distinctive landscape of Sydhavnstippen. Over the course of three hours, choreographer and dancer Anna Pehrsson, together with a group of dancers, creates an open field of movement and stillness that unfolds in dialogue with the site.

Sydhavnstippen is a human-made landscape, shaped by waste and landfill between 1945 and 1973, and today a living ecosystem. Here, the fleeting present of dance is interwoven with deep time and geological slowness. Bodies move in relation to soil, vegetation, and wind, mirroring the processes of accumulation, decay, and transformation that have shaped the landscape.

*Mountainbuilding* invites the audience into a sensory experience and a quiet reflection on what connects us all: body and landscape, human action and nature’s ongoing transformation.

title: The Lamenters — dancing into tears
description:
Through embodied, ritual-based practices, each performer unfolds their own lament – a personal and ritualised song of mourning in which sorrow, loss, and longing are given form and transformed into movement. Here, the body functions as a site of transformation. Crying is not understood as complaint or weakness, but as an opening: a shared space where vulnerability is witnessed, amplified, and allowed to generate new connections.

Drawing on historical lament traditions that reach back to antiquity – where song and ritual have been used to express grief, longing, gratitude, and care – *The Lamenters* revitalises the genre as a physical and sensorial language that extends beyond words. Tears become a connective force between the individual and the collective, activating a shared bodily resonance between performers and audience.

Developed for the landscape of Sydhavnstippen, where vegetation pushes through discarded building materials and remnants of human-made structures, the performance responds to each site as a living ritual. The surroundings mirror the logic of crying itself: something unpredictable, uncontrollable, and transformative, breaking through that which otherwise holds us together.

*The Lamenters* is a poetic and political ensemble work about witnessing one another in tears – and about the transformative potential that emerges when what breaks through is allowed to be seen, shared, and carried collectively.

title: double bill: velvet & _plex
description:
Claire Vivianne Sobottke og Alma Toaspern åbner årets KIN FESTIVAL 2026. Vi præsenterer to værker af Claire Vivenne Sobottke og Alma Toaspern, der på hver sin måde undersøger kroppens natur. I hhv. et kostrueret vildnis og et lydunivers af 1.000 pophits forsøger de at skabe mening i kaosset. De to værker åbner årets KIN FESTIVAL

### **Velvet**

Velveter en sanselig undersøgelse af den feminine krops historie.

Claire Vivianne Sobottkes soloværk udfolder sig i en konstrueret miniaturehave - af jord, planter og sten - og undersøger ensomhed og seksualitet. Sobottke konstruerer en række portrætter af skabninger, der bevæger sig i disse landskaber og udfordrer normer for måder at se og tænke verden på. *Velvet* bevæger sig mellem det poetiske og det irrationelle for både at konstruere og dekonstruere legemliggørelser og identiteter – herunder idéen om det feminine og kvindelige.

### **\_plex**

I \_\_plex dykker koreograf Alma Toaspern ned i popkulturens sanselige og følelsesmæssige intensitet. Inspireret af musiker John Oswalds visionære album *Plexure* (1993) – en kollage af over 1.000 pophits – omsætter værket lydens fragmenter til bevægelse, rytme og nærvær. Gennem kroppen undersøges, hvordan vi navigerer i en tid præget af konstant mediestøj, algoritmisk remixkultur og flygtige impulser.

Kraftfuldt, humoristisk og overvældende søger hver bevægelse at skabe mening midt i overstimuleringen – et glimt af klarhed i kaos.

#### Velvet og \_plex er en del af årets yes — dance biennale. Læs mere **her.**

title: New Nordic Poling
description:
New Nordic Poling (NNP) is a movement of eight activist pole dancers who “poles 4 change.”

The performance unfolds at NNP’s annual recruitment meeting, where their documentary *Radical Embrace* has its premiere. The audience follows the movement as they create banners, challenge Extinction Rebellion on May 1st in Copenhagen’s Sydhavn, and travel to Svalbard to “Pole 4 the melting pole” with their new portable pole.

In an intimate, mildly interactive setup- blending live action with film screening - the audience follows NNP’s highs and lows. NNP investigates power structures and plans happenings, but faces unexpected challenges of activist group work. What does it mean to practice what you preach? And when does a group become a group?

New Nordic Poling is about hopelessness, resilience, and the (im)possible pursuit of collective meaning.

title: BOAT TOURS NHZ (New Harbour Zones) // CPH STAGE
description:
#### NB! The performance will be performed in English.

Hop on board when the large-scale performance BOAT TOURS NHZ (New Harbour Zones) once again takes over the Copenhagen harbour.

The performance is built around a boat tour on a tourist vessel to hidden locations, where we experience Copenhagen’s waterfront with new eyes. Drawing on, among other things, the history of Sydhavn, a future scenario is staged. Everyone is invited on an adventure where the unexpected unfolds — both on land and at sea.

In a hybrid of choreography, performance, text and music, BOAT TOURS NHZ (New Harbour Zones) explores both the contemporary harbourfront and the old royal, military and merchant port. The performance launches into a fantasy of the harbour of the future, playing with the ideas and stories that the harbour represents. With humour and gravitas, it brings the ideological and political battles over the city into play.

Captain Atlas and his crew — Agent Neptune, Agent Jupiter and Agent Polaris — look forward to welcoming you on board!

#### The performance is curated by CPH STAGE and presented in collaboration with PUBLIC PERFORMANCE and Sydhavn Teater.

title: åben torsdag — musikbanko
description:
**MUSIC BINGO**

This evening, we're going to be immersed in the best songs from the brilliant decade of the 90's - from Natalie Imbruglia, to Donna Lewis, to Erykah Badu, Cher, George Michael, and Radiohead... There are great prizes to win, so gather a group and come along!

It’s free to join, but hurry and book your spot — space is limited!

**About Open Thursday**

Once a month on a Thursday evening, Sydhavn Teater invites everyone who feels like it to join us for nights filled with music, words, dance, movement, and time together. Each event is different, but most of it is rooted in everything that can happen on a stage.

It's free to join, but space is limited — so if you want to secure a spot, you can sign up now.

We look forward to seeing you!

title: åben torsdag — fri scene
description:
At **FREE STAGE**, anyone who feels like it – regardless of experience – can share something with a small audience. It could be a song you’ve written, a cover that you want to sing, a bit of stand-up material, a monologue, a dance, a story, a performance, or something completely different. It can be brand new and unfinished, or something you’ve been working on for a long time – everything is welcome.

**WHAT YOU DO:**

* If you want to show something, write a short text about your idea (max. 200 words) OR make a short video, where you tell us what you’d like to show (max 2 minutes)
* Write a short text about yourself (max. 100 words) OR make a short video or recording where you tell us about yourself (max. 1 min).

Send the above to Sophie Grodin before 7th May 2026 at [sophie@sydhavnteater.dk](mailto:sophie@sydhavnteater.dk)

At each FREE STAGE, a maximum of 4 people or groups can show something that lasts a maximum of 15 minutes. This also means that if too many people have registered on the same evening, you may be offered to show something at a later FREE STAGE (you can see the remaining dates of the year in our calendar).

FREE STAGE takes place at our intimate stage, the ANNEX scene, at Borbjergsvej 44 in Sydhavn.

If you want to participate as an audience member, you don’t need to do anything other than book a ticket and show up! Book your ticket via the ‘buy ticket link’ at the top of the page (here you will also be able to see all the Open Thursday dates)

You will most likely experience something, that has never been shared with an audience before.

title: TEATERVÆRKSTED
description:
Every Tuesday from 3:00 PM — 5:00 PM, Sydhavn Teater invites you to our ANNEX STAGE at Borgbjergsvej 44 for an informal theatre workshop for adults (16+) where we explore storytelling, listening, improvisation, movement, and much more — together.

**Everyone is welcome!** Whether you already love theatre or are just curious to see what it’s all about. You can come once or ten times — it’s entirely up to you.

We won’t be performing on stage, but instead working together in a safe and creative space through various activities inspired by the world of theatre.

**Participation is free**, and we’d love it if you signed up via the link below so we know how much coffee and tea to brew — but you’re also welcome to just show up on the day.

Sophie Grodin from Sydhavn Teater will lead the activities and make sure everyone feels included. Sophie has many years of experience teaching theatre techniques and is used to both performing and directing others.

**Questions?**  
Send an email to Sophie Grodin at sophie@sydhavnteater.dk

title: BENCHED
description:
Take a seat! Sit down! You’re benched! Taken out! Put together! Are you sitting in the right place?

A raw and honest story about life’s big — and very small — questions. About settling in. About daring to sit crooked, to sit across, to be placed, replaced, or benched. About taking, making, and giving space — and hopefully remembering to do it before you fall flat on your ass where the bench ends.

BENCHED has captivated and won over more than 10,000 enthusiastic audience members during its many tours, which have taken the performance across Denmark, Sweden, Turkey, Canada, Italy, Finland, Germany, and Hong Kong.

title: ISNÆTTER
description:
ISNÆTTER is a fusion of dance and theatre — a performance about power and desire, based on author Anna Kavan’s cult novel *ICE* from 1967.

Playwright Amanda Ginman — Reumert Award winner for *Mørkt Forår* — and acclaimed choreographer Tina Tarpgaard join forces in a performance where dance, text, and sound create an intense and physical portrait of our present.

In a world covered in ice, an unnamed male narrator pursues a mysterious woman — yet she continually escapes him. He shapes his own reality and moves through the world driven by fascination, desire, jealousy, and an overwhelming urge for control. The longer he chases her, the more reality and fantasy begin to merge — and fracture.

Is he her savior?

**ISNÆTTER** is nightmare-ish sci-fi, 1960s road movie, and psychedelic dream logic — a hypnotic journey through obsession, power, and downfall, where the boundary between reality and hallucination dissolves, like everything else. Like a nightmare you cannot wake from.

The longer he chases her, the more reality and fantasy begin to merge — and fracture.

Is he her savior?

**ISNÆTTER** is nightmare-ish sci-fi, 1960s road movie, and psychedelic dream logic — a hypnotic journey through obsession, power, and downfall, where the boundary between reality and hallucination dissolves, like everything else. Like a nightmare you cannot wake from.

title: From the finite to the infinite to the finite to the...
description:
Under the open sky, carried by sound and text, the audience meets a woman moving through the former landfill in Valby Park — a place where the outer terrain intertwines with inner memories. The woman appears as some kind of shadow figure, bearing traces of something familiar to us all. Gradually, she dissolves the sense of time and place.

FROM THE FINITE TO THE INFINITE TO THE FINITE TO THE… is a choreographed walk, in which the two artists Margrét Sara Guðjónsdóttir and Marie Topp invite the audience into their shared fascination and long-standing artistic exploration of an alternative experience of time — a temporality that is felt rather than understood. The music is composed by British cellist and composer Lucy Railton.
